      <title>Victor Wembanyama: 'I Do Care Deeply' About Winning NBA MVP</title>
      <link>https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/284848/Victor-Wembanyama-I-Do-Care-Deeply-About-Winning-NBA-MVP</link>
      <description><p>Victor Wembanyama reiterated his MVP intentions plainly Wednesday, telling ESPN he is actively chasing the award. The declaration came after a commanding 41-point, 18-rebound performance in San Antonio's 127-113 road victory over Golden State.</p>
<p>"I do care deeply about it," Wembanyama told ESPN's Scott Van Pelt. "I think that of the greats that are in the Hall of Fame -- or the best of all time -- they have fought and grabbed everything they could grab early on in their career. If I want to make my spot among the greats, I got to try to not miss any occasion to put my name up there."</p>
<p>If Wembanyama were to win the award, he would be <a href="https://basketball.realgm.com/nba/awards/by-type">the youngest MVP in NBA history</a> ahead of Derrick Rose who was 22 years and 211 days when he won in 2011. Wembanyama will be approximately 22 years and 130 days old in May. Wes Unseld also won the award when he was 22 years old and was in his rookie season.</p>
<p>The Spurs have won 10 consecutive games overall and 15 straight contests in which Wembanyama has played. He has averaged 27.9 points and 12.3 rebounds during that run.</p>
<p>Wembanyama has also recorded at least 15 rebounds in five straight games, the longest such stretch by a Spurs player since Dennis Rodman in 1995, surpassing benchmarks set by both Tim Duncan and David Robinson.</p>
<p>"Wow," Wembanyama said of the Rodman comparison. "I watched a lot of highlights [of Rodman], mostly on the Bulls than anything else. They don't make them like [him] anymore."</p>
<p>San Antonio sits at 58-18, trailing the Oklahoma City Thunder (60-16) by two games for the top Western Conference seed. The Spurs hold the tiebreaker with a 4-1 head-to-head advantage and six games remaining.</p>
<p>Defending winner Shai Gilgeous-Alexander remains <a href="https://basketball.realgm.com/betting/news/nba-mvp-betting-odds/">the MVP award betting favorite</a>. Steve Kerr, however, offered strong praise for Wembanyama following Wednesday's defeat.</p>
<p>"He's got unbelievable confidence now," Kerr said. "He looks like he knows exactly what to do on both ends. His first couple years, he looked young at times. He doesn't look young anymore. He just looks like he knows exactly what he's doing. Dominated the glass. There's plays you can't do anything. Several lobs where it looked like a Nerf hoop. He's brilliant."</p>
<p>Regardless of the MVP outcome, Wembanyama is widely viewed as <a href="https://basketball.realgm.com/nba/awards/by-type/Defensive-Player-of-the-Year/2">a certain first-time Defensive Player of the Year winner</a>. Wembanyama would become the fifth different player to win the award over the past five seasons.</p></description>
      <pubDate>Thu, 02 Apr 2026 10:52:44 EDT</pubDate>
      <guid>https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/284848/Victor-Wembanyama-I-Do-Care-Deeply-About-Winning-NBA-MVP</guid>
      <media:thumbnail url="https://basketball.realgm.com/images/nba/4.2/wiretap/photos/2006/Wembanyama_Victor_sas_240516.jpg" />
   </item>
   <item>
      <title>NBA Hasn't Set Timeline To Conclude Clippers' Salary Cap Investigation</title>
      <link>https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/284853/NBA-Hasnt-Set-Timeline-To-Conclude-Clippers-Salary-Cap-Investigation</link>
      <description><p>The NBA's investigation into alleged salary cap circumvention involving the LA Clippers and Kawhi Leonard has no set end date, with interviews still ongoing nearly seven months after the probe began.</p>
<p>"Wachtell's investigation is ongoing," an NBA spokesperson said. "There is more work to be done and no set timeline."</p>
<p>The inquiry, led by law firm Wachtell, Lipton, Rosen & Katz and attorney David Anders, centers on a $28 million endorsement deal between Leonard and Aspiration, a now-bankrupt green banking company in which Clippers owner Steve Ballmer had invested. Sources with knowledge of the matter confirmed that investigators are still conducting interviews with Clippers officials and former Aspiration employees familiar with the sponsorship arrangement. It remains unclear whether Leonard himself, or anyone in his circle, has been interviewed.</p>
<p>The investigation follows reporting by Pablo Torre, whose work cited internal documents showing Ballmer invested $50 million in Aspiration through his personal LLC in September 2021. That same month, the Clippers signed a $300 million deal with Aspiration. Six months later, Aspiration executed the endorsement agreement with Leonard.</p>
<p>Ballmer has acknowledged introducing Leonard to Aspiration but denied directing the company to sign him.</p>
<p>"We just can't be involved," Ballmer told ESPN. "We made an introduction."</p>
<p>Both Leonard and Clippers president of basketball operations Lawrence Frank have publicly welcomed the investigation and denied any wrongdoing.</p>
<p>"We are glad there's an investigation, and we welcome it," Frank said at the team's media day. "We appreciate that there'll be a clear-eyed look at these allegations, and we're eager for the truth to come out."</p>
<p>Leonard was equally direct.</p>
<p>"None of us did any wrongdoing," said Leonard. "That's it. We invite the investigations. It's not going to be a distraction for me or the rest of the team."</p>
<p>Former Aspiration co-founder and CEO Andrei Cherny also denied the circumvention allegations, stating the deal included extensive obligations for Leonard and followed thorough internal deliberation. However, three former senior executives chief financial officer Rojeh Avanesian, chief operating officer Mike Shuckerow and chief technology officer Eric Anderson issued a joint statement contradicting that account.</p>
<p>"The team expressed concerns at the time regarding the high cost of the arrangement and its lack of alignment with Aspiration's brand and business strategy," the executives said. "In our judgement, the Leonard Deal was not in the company's best interest. It was strategically difficult to justify then, and it remains so today."</p>
<p>ESPN separately obtained Leonard's 19-page contract with Aspiration and showed relevant language to five player agents and a National Basketball Players Association source familiar with endorsement agreements. The consensus among those sources was that the contract contained largely standard provisions.</p>
<p>"This is standard. Nothing unusual here," said one agent. A second added: "There's nothing in there that jumps out to me." Two agents flagged the contract's "beliefs" clause - which allows Leonard to decline obligations inconsistent with his personal values - as unusually broad, with one calling it "an amazing negotiation and/or an inexperienced lawyer on behalf of Aspiration."</p>
<p>The NBPA source reached the same conclusion.</p>
<p>"There is nothing in that contract that is inconsistent with the regular course of business," the source told ESPN, noting the beliefs clause was the only notable provision.</p>
<p>The five agents and the NBPA source collectively found nothing in the contract itself suggesting the deal was structured to circumvent the salary cap.</p>
<p>Should Wachtell Lipton's findings merit discipline, NBA Commissioner Adam Silver would not act unilaterally. Under the league's collective bargaining agreement, Silver would present the firm's conclusions to a neutral arbitrator jointly appointed by the NBA and the NBPA. That arbitrator would determine whether sufficient evidence exists to authorize penalties.</p>
<p>The investigation's complexity has drawn acknowledgment from Silver himself. At All-Star Weekend, hosted by the Clippers, he noted the challenges involved</p>
<p>"You have a company in bankruptcy. You have thousands of documents, multiple witnesses that have been needed to be interviewed." Silver added that the Clippers have been "fully cooperative."</p>
<p>A previous Wachtell Lipton investigation into former Phoenix Suns owner Robert Sarver, also led by Anders, involved 320 interviews and more than 80,000 documents reviewed, taking 10 months to conclude. Aspiration founder Joseph Sanberg pleaded guilty to two counts of wire fraud in August 2025 and is scheduled for sentencing April 27 in Los Angeles federal court.</p></description>
      <pubDate>Thu, 02 Apr 2026 12:34:06 EDT</pubDate>
      <guid>https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/284853/NBA-Hasnt-Set-Timeline-To-Conclude-Clippers-Salary-Cap-Investigation</guid>
      <media:thumbnail url="https://basketball.realgm.com/images/nba/4.2/wiretap/photos/2006/Leonard_Kawhi_lac_250423.jpg" />

   <item>
      <title>Luka Doncic, Jalen Johnson Named NBA Players Of March</title>
      <link>https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/284856/Luka-Doncic-Jalen-Johnson-Named-NBA-Players-Of-March</link>
      <description><p>Luka Doncic and Jalen Johnson have been named the <a href="https://basketball.realgm.com/nba/awards/by-type/Player-of-the-Month/29">NBA's Players of the Month</a> for March.</p>
<p>Doncic averaged 37.5 points, 8.0 rebounds and 7.4 assists as the Los Angeles Lakers went 15-2. Doncic previously won the award in January.</p>
<p>Johnson averaged 22.4 points, 8.5 rebounds and 8.5 assists as the Atlanta Hawks went 13-2. This is the first time Johnson has won Player of the Month in his career.</p></description>
      <pubDate>Thu, 02 Apr 2026 15:04:53 EDT</pubDate>
      <guid>https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/284856/Luka-Doncic-Jalen-Johnson-Named-NBA-Players-Of-March</guid>

   <item>
      <title>Jalen Duren, Pistons Both Say They Want Long-Term Deal In Offseason</title>
      <link>https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/284864/Jalen-Duren-Pistons-Both-Say-They-Want-Long-Term-Deal-In-Offseason</link>
      <description><p>Jalen Duren and the Detroit Pistons both say they want a long-term partnership despite failing to reach a contract extension by the deadline last October, making the 22-year-old center a restricted free agent this summer. Both sides insist the impasse was purely a business matter.</p>
<p>"I mean, it was what it was," Duren said. "I think it's tough because there is a business aspect to it. Like I said, I would love to be a Piston for life. It was nothing personal. Nothing I took personal with the organization or anything. It was just business. They felt one way, I felt another. And at that moment we couldn't come to an agreement."</p>
<p>General manager Trajan Langdon echoed that sentiment from the organization's side.</p>
<p>"We want him to be a Piston for life, too," Langdon said. "It had nothing to do with what he had or hadn't done."</p>
<p>The contract situation looms over what has otherwise been a breakthrough season for Duren. He made his first All-Star team in February and is posting career highs across the board, averaging 19.5 points on 64.7 percent shooting and nearly 11 rebounds per game. He ranks among the frontrunners for the league's Most Improved Player award.</p>
<p>Duren has also shouldered the offensive load during Cade Cunningham's eight-game absence due to a collapsed lung, averaging 23.4 points, 10.5 rebounds and 1.8 assists while guiding Detroit to a 6-2 record. The Pistons clinched the Central Division title Tuesday and sit atop the Eastern Conference with six games remaining.</p>
<p>Cunningham and Duren have built one of the league's most promising young duos, traveling together to Italy last summer and deepening a bond that traces back to their AAU and USA Basketball days. Duren has drawn comparisons to the Julian Edelman-Tom Brady dynamic in terms of his deliberate investment in building chemistry with his star teammate.</p>
<p>With the playoffs approaching as the East's top seed, Duren's focus remains on the present despite the unresolved contract situation.</p>
<p>"I just want this team to use its full potential," Duren said. "I want to come out of this season thinking, 'Yeah. We did everything that we wanted to do.'"</p></description>
      <pubDate>Thu, 02 Apr 2026 22:10:49 EDT</pubDate>
      <guid>https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/284864/Jalen-Duren-Pistons-Both-Say-They-Want-Long-Term-Deal-In-Offseason</guid>
      <media:thumbnail url="https://basketball.realgm.com/images/nba/4.2/wiretap/photos/2006/Duren_Jalen_det_251210.jpg" />
   </item>

      <title>Tre Scott, Nets Sign 10-Day Hardship Contract</title>
      <link>https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/284847/Tre-Scott-Nets-Sign-10-Day-Hardship-Contract</link>
      <description><p>Tre Scott and the Brooklyn Nets have signed a 10-day hardship deal.</p>
<p><a href="https://basketball.realgm.com/player/Tre-Scott/Summary/84083">Scott has appeared in 47 games this season with the Long Island Nets</a>, averaging 12.0 points, 4.8 rebounds and 2.0 assists in 28.1 minutes. Scott was with the Nets for training camp.</p>
<p>Scott, a 6'8 small forward, went undrafted out of Cincinnati in 2020.</p>
<p>Danny Wolf is likely to miss the remainder of the season with a sprained ankle.</p></description>
      <pubDate>Thu, 02 Apr 2026 10:44:16 EDT</pubDate>

   <item>
      <title>NBA Brainstormed Fantasy Football-Style 'Draft Credit' System Before Rejecting Concept</title>
      <link>https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/284858/NBA-Brainstormed-Fantasy-Football-Style-Draft-Credit-System-Before-Rejecting-Concept</link>
      <description><p>The NBA considered but ultimately set aside a concept known as the "draft credit" system during internal brainstorming sessions, sources told The Stein Line. The idea was discussed before <a href="https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/284708/NBA-Presented-Three-Draft-Lottery-Reform-Options-To-Board-Of-Governors">preferred reform concepts were presented to all 30 teams</a> at the recent Board of Governors meeting in Manhattan.</p>
<p>The system would have severed the direct link between regular-season record and draft position entirely, a fundamental departure from the current lottery structure.</p>
<p>Under the concept, each franchise would receive an equal allotment of draft credits, 100 for simplicity's sake, rather than traditional first- and second-round picks. Teams would then bid those credits in an auction format, starting with the No. 1 overall selection and proceeding down the board.</p>
<p>The trade implications would have been equally significant. Instead of exchanging actual draft picks for veteran players, front offices could offer portions of their credit allotment, say 40 of 100 credits, to acquiring teams. Those teams could then accumulate credits to outbid rivals for top selections.</p>
<p>The mechanism drew comparisons to waiver wire budgets in fantasy football, where teams bid finite currency on available players rather than operating on a fixed priority order.</p>
<p><a href="https://www.espn.com/nba/draft2015/insider/story/_/id/12912177/nba-draft-abolish-draft">Amin Elhassan proposed a similar system that abolished the draft as early as 2015</a>.</p>
<p>The concept ultimately failed to gain serious traction compared to less disruptive proposals now under active league consideration, specifically adding Play-In teams to the lottery pool and further flattening lottery odds among non-playoff clubs.</p>
<p>Those measures remain in genuine contention for adoption in May as <a href="https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/284258/Adam-Silver-Pledges-Substantial-Draft-Lottery-Changes-To-Combat-Tanking">Adam Silver has pledged substantial lottery changes to combat tanking</a>.</p></description>
      <pubDate>Thu, 02 Apr 2026 15:32:54 EDT</pubDate>
      <guid>https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/284858/NBA-Brainstormed-Fantasy-Football-Style-Draft-Credit-System-Before-Rejecting-Concept</guid>
      <media:thumbnail url="https://basketball.realgm.com/images/nba/4.2/wiretap/photos/2006/Silver_Adam_nba_230403.jpg" />
   </item>
   <item>
      <title>Darius Acuff Closing Gap On Darryn Peterson At Guard On NBA Draft Boards</title>
      <link>https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/284857/Darius-Acuff-Closing-Gap-On-Darryn-Peterson-At-Guard-On-NBA-Draft-Boards</link>
      <description><p>Arkansas freshman Darius Acuff is mounting a serious challenge to Darryn Peterson to be the top-ranked guard prospect in the 2026 NBA Draft after polling across the league. Several scouts now rank Acuff ahead of Peterson among guards following dominant performances in both the SEC and NCAA Tournaments.</p>
<p>"Acuff definitely put himself even further on the map," said one veteran college scout.</p>
<p>Acuff's case is bolstered by an endorsement from Arkansas coach John Calipari, who has stated the 6'3" guard is <a href="https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/284702/John-Calipari-Puts-Darius-Acuff-Jr-Among-Elite-Guards-He-Has-Previously-Sent-To-NBA">as talented as any freshman guard he has coached</a>, which is a list that includes Derrick Rose, Shai Gilgeous-Alexander and John Wall.</p>
<p>Acuff averaged 23.5 points and 6.4 assists per game this season, becoming just the second player in SEC history, joining Pete Maravich in 1969-70, to lead the conference in both categories in the same season. He also set Arkansas single-season records in both points (845) and assists (232).</p>
<p><a href="https://basketball.realgm.com/nba/draft/prospects/stats">Among 2026 NBA draft prospects</a>, Acuff ranked second in scoring behind A.J. Dybantsa and second in assists behind Braden Smith.</p>
<p>Acuff shot 48.4 percent from the field, 44.0 percent from three and 80.9 percent from the free-throw line, elite efficiency for a freshman guard. Some evaluators have raised concerns about his size and defensive ability at the next level, though Calipari has pushed back firmly on both counts.</p>
<p>Peterson still holds the top spot among guards on most boards, but the gap between the two has narrowed following their performances in the NCAA Tournament. </p></description>
      <pubDate>Thu, 02 Apr 2026 15:24:59 EDT</pubDate>
      <guid>https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/284857/Darius-Acuff-Closing-Gap-On-Darryn-Peterson-At-Guard-On-NBA-Draft-Boards</guid>
      <media:thumbnail url="https://basketball.realgm.com/images/nba/4.2/wiretap/photos/2006/Acuff_Darius_ark_260316.jpg" />
   </item>
   <item>
      <title>Cade Cunningham Out At Least One More Week, Will Lose Award Eligibility</title>
      <link>https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/284859/Cade-Cunningham-Out-At-Least-One-More-Week-Will-Lose-Award-Eligibility</link>
      <description><p>Detroit Pistons guard Cade Cunningham continues to progress in his return from a left lung pneumothorax, the team announced Thursday. He will be re-evaluated in one week, ruling him out for at least four more games.</p>
<p>The timeline makes Cunningham ineligible for end-of-season honors including MVP and All-NBA teams. He suffered the injury in the first quarter of Detroit's win over Washington on March 17 and has missed eight games since. Cunningham was considered a lock to make an All-NBA team had he achieved eligibility. </p>
<p>Cunningham made his first bench appearance since the injury during Tuesday's win over the Toronto Raptors, a game in which the Pistons clinched the Central Division title for the first time since 2008.</p>
<p>The 24-year-old All-Star has averaged 24.5 points, 9.9 assists, 5.6 rebounds, 1.5 steals and 0.9 blocks across 61 games this season, with his assists, steals and blocks all representing career highs. He was firmly in the MVP conversation before being sidelined.</p>
<p>His potential award ineligibility has renewed debate over the NBA's 65-game rule. <a href="https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/284656/NBPA-Demands-Reform-Of-65-Game-Rule-As-Award-Eligibility-Threatens-NBAs-Top-Stars">The National Basketball Players Association called for the rule to be addressed last week</a>.</p>
<p>"Cade Cunningham's potential ineligibility for postseason awards after a career-defining season is a clear indictment of the 65-game rule and yet another example of why it must be abolished or reformed to create an exception for significant injuries," an NBPA spokesperson said. "Since its implementation, far too many deserving players have been unfairly disqualified from end-of-season honors by this arbitrary and overly rigid quota."</p>
<p><a href="https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/284681/Adam-Silver-Believes-65-Game-Rule-Working-NBA-Not-Currently-Willing-To-Reassess">Adam Silver quickly responded to the critique that he believes the 65-game minimum is having the desired effect</a> while expressing empathy for Cunningham's situation.</p>
<p>Detroit stands at 55-21 and sits two wins from securing the best record in the Eastern Conference for the first time since 2007. The Pistons have gone 6-2 during Cunningham's absence.</p></description>
      <pubDate>Thu, 02 Apr 2026 15:38:14 EDT</pubDate>
      <guid>https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/284859/Cade-Cunningham-Out-At-Least-One-More-Week-Will-Lose-Award-Eligibility</guid>
      <media:thumbnail url="https://basketball.realgm.com/images/nba/4.2/wiretap/photos/2006/Cunningham_Cade_det_250423.jpg" />
   </item>
   <item>
      <title>LeBron James Says He Might Have Pulled An 'Eli Manning' If Grizzlies Won 2003 Lottery</title>
      <link>https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/284861/LeBron-James-Says-He-Might-Have-Pulled-An-Eli-Manning-If-Grizzlies-Won-2003-Lottery</link>
      <description><p>LeBron James suggested he may have refused to play for the Memphis Grizzlies had they won <a href="https://basketball.realgm.com/nba/draft/lottery_results/2003">the 2003 NBA Draft lottery</a>, comparing a hypothetical holdout to Eli Manning's refusal to suit up for the San Diego Chargers in the 2004 NFL Draft. </p>
<p>"Their only chance was in 2003 if they would've won the lottery to get me," James said. "And I might've pulled an Eli Manning and not shown up."</p>
<p>The Cleveland Cavaliers won the lottery in 2003 and selected James, where he remained until exiting to the Miami Heat during his 2010 free agency. The Grizzlies jumped up from sixth to second, but their draft pick still went to the Detroit Pistons as it was only top-1 protected. Memphis, who was represented on the dais by Jerry West, lost out on the opportunity to select Dwyane Wade, Chris Bosh or Darko Milicic. </p>
<p>The comments came during a April 2 appearance on the "Bob Does Sports" YouTube channel, where the Lakers star was discussing the difficulties of NBA travel before setting his sights on Memphis.</p>
<p>James did not stop at the Manning comparison as he also called openly for the Grizzlies to relocate to Nashville.</p>
<p>"Memphis on a random ass Thursday," James said. "I'm not like the first guy to even talk about it in the NBA. We all, like, 'You guys have to move. Just go over to Nashville. You got Vanderbilt over there, you got the NASCAR, you got a stadium. Don't they got a hockey team? Like, they've got everything."</p>
<p>James joins a growing list of prominent voices pushing the same narrative. J.J. Redick apologized in 2023 after the topic surfaced on his podcast. Draymond Green called for the move on his own podcast in March. ESPN's Stephen A. Smith has drawn repeated backlash from Memphis residents for similar remarks, recently suggesting Nashville could land a team through NBA expansion.</p>
<p>The relocation debate remains deeply contentious in Memphis.</p></description>
      <pubDate>Thu, 02 Apr 2026 17:12:22 EDT</pubDate>
      <guid>https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/284861/LeBron-James-Says-He-Might-Have-Pulled-An-Eli-Manning-If-Grizzlies-Won-2003-Lottery</guid>
      <media:thumbnail url="https://basketball.realgm.com/images/nba/4.2/wiretap/photos/2006/James_LeBron_lal_240203.jpg" />
   </item>
   <item>
      <title>Bez Mbeng, Jazz Signing Rest Of Season Contract</title>
      <link>https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/284845/Bez-Mbeng-Jazz-Signing-Rest-Of-Season-Contract</link>
      <description><p>The Utah Jazz and guard Bez Mbeng are signing a rest-of-season contract. Mbeng completed two 10 Day deals with Utah. In order to keep the rookie guard on the roster, the Jazz now have to sign him for the remainder of the season.</p>
<p>Mbeng has appeared in 11 games across his two 10 Day contracts with Utah. The 6-foot-4 guard has averaged 5.0 points, 3.4 rebounds, 3.4 assists and 1.7 steals in 29.9 minutes per game.</p></description>

   <item>
      <title>Arizona's Tommy Lloyd: 'I'm Not A Multitasker' Amid North Carolina Head Coach Rumors</title>
      <link>https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/284865/Arizonas-Tommy-Lloyd-Im-Not-A-Multitasker-Amid-North-Carolina-Head-Coach-Rumors</link>
      <description><p>Arizona head coach Tommy Lloyd declined to engage with speculation linking him to the North Carolina vacancy Thursday, saying his complete attention belongs to the Wildcats ahead of Saturday's Final Four matchup against Michigan.</p>
<p>"I'm a simple guy. I'm kind of just one thing at a time. I'm not a multitasker," Lloyd said at his pregame news conference. "You can ask my wife. So, I'm 100% locked in on Arizona basketball right now, and I'm excited to see what this team can do. I have a real strong belief in this team and this team deserves my full attention, so that's what I'm giving."</p>
<p>North Carolina fired Hubert Davis last month, creating one of college basketball's premier vacancies. Lloyd, Chicago Bulls coach Billy Donovan and Michigan's Dusty May have all been identified as top candidates. The fact that UNC has yet to name a replacement has fueled speculation that its primary target remains active in the tournament or in the NBA.</p>
<p>Lloyd's buyout reportedly dropped from $11 million to $9 million Thursday. The financial picture at Chapel Hill is complex, the Tar Heels still owe Davis more than $5 million, have committed tens of millions to coach Bill Belichick and the football program, and would face significant salary demands from any top coaching hire. Lloyd currently earns $5.3 million annually, while the five highest-paid college coaches in the country make between $6 million and $9 million.</p>
<p>In just his fifth season leading Arizona, Lloyd has guided the Wildcats to their first Final Four appearance since 1997. The program reached college basketball's premier event with a deep eight-man rotation featuring Big 12 Player of the Year Jaden Bradley and projected first-round picks Koa Peat and Brayden Burries, both freshmen. Arizona and Michigan rank first and second respectively in KenPom's national rankings.</p>
<p>Lloyd remained consistent when pressed further on the subject.</p>
<p>"Listen, I've got my full focus on this team. Nothing is distracting me," he said. "And that's just how I decided to approach it."</p></description>
      <pubDate>Thu, 02 Apr 2026 22:17:27 EDT</pubDate>
      <guid>https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/284865/Arizonas-Tommy-Lloyd-Im-Not-A-Multitasker-Amid-North-Carolina-Head-Coach-Rumors</guid>
      <media:thumbnail url="https://basketball.realgm.com/images/nba/4.2/wiretap/photos/2006/Lloyd_Tommy_az_260316.jpg" />
   </item>
   <item>
      <title>Kansas Center Flory Bidunga To Declare For NBA Draft, Enter Transfer Portal</title>
      <link>https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/284866/Kansas-Center-Flory-Bidunga-To-Declare-For-NBA-Draft-Enter-Transfer-Portal</link>
      <description><p>Kansas sophomore center Flory Bidunga intends to declare for the NBA Draft while maintaining his college eligibility, sources told ESPN Thursday. Bidunga will also enter the NCAA transfer portal, preserving his option to return to college basketball.</p>
<p>Bidunga would immediately rank as the top available player among the more than 800 athletes who have declared for the portal so far, according to ESPN's Jeff Borzello. The portal formally opens Tuesday, the day after the NCAA championship game.</p>
<p>On the professional side, ESPN's Jeremy Woo projects Bidunga as a second-round pick at No. 44 in the current draft class.</p>
<p>The 6-foot-10, 235-pound big man averaged 13.3 points and 9.0 rebounds per game for the Jayhawks this season, finishing third in the Big 12 in rebounding. He led the conference in both blocks (2.6 per game) and field goal percentage, connecting on 64 percent of his attempts, nearly seven points above the next closest qualifier.</p>
<p>Bidunga was also a candidate for national defensive player of the year honors, underlining his two-way impact at the collegiate level.</p>
<p>Should he opt to remain in college for his junior season, Bidunga figures to be among the most coveted transfer targets in the country. Interior size and production command a significant premium on the current transfer market, and few players in the portal can match his combination of rim protection and finishing ability.</p></description>

      <title>Penny Hardaway Names Gabriel Harris First General Manager At Memphis</title>
      <link>https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/284860/Penny-Hardaway-Names-Gabriel-Harris-First-General-Manager-At-Memphis</link>
      <description><p>University of Memphis men's basketball coach Penny Hardaway has hired Gabriel Harris as the program's first-ever general manager, the school announced Thursday. Harris comes to Memphis after serving as general manager of the Stockton Kings, the NBA G League affiliate of the Sacramento Kings.</p>
<p>"We are thrilled to welcome Gabriel Harris to Memphis," Hardaway said. "Gabriel brings a modern approach and new perspective to roster management, along with valuable experience at the professional level. His leadership and vision will be instrumental as we continue to build a championship-caliber program."</p>
<p>Harris guided Stockton to the 2025 NBA G League Championship and spent three seasons with the organization, the last two as general manager after joining as assistant GM prior to the 2023-24 season. He helped construct a roster that produced the G League's best regular-season record at 24-10 during his time as assistant general manager.</p>
<p>"I'm excited for this next chapter with the University of Memphis," Harris said. "The history, expectations, and tradition of Memphis basketball are what drew me to this opportunity, and I look forward to working with Coach Hardaway to help build a highly successful program."</p>
<p>Multiple players advanced to the NBA under Harris's watch, including Mason Jones, who earned a two-way contract with Sacramento, Jaylen Nowell, who signed with both the Memphis Grizzlies and Detroit Pistons, and Keon Ellis, whose two-way deal was converted to a standard NBA contract with the Kings.</p>
<p>In his new role, Harris will oversee roster management, recruiting operations, revenue share strategy and player personnel evaluation. Prior to his time in Stockton, he spent nine years at Adidas as a marketing executive across the Yeezy, grassroots basketball and footwear divisions.</p>
<p>A Stanford graduate, Harris played four years of college basketball under coach Johnny Dawkins from 2009-12, earning Pac-12 All-Academic Honorable Mention honors and a 2012 NIT championship. He holds degrees in behavioral psychology and digital communication, along with an Applied Data Science certificate from MIT.</p></description>

      <description><p>Steph Curry has made it a goal to return to the Golden State Warriors' lineup from a knee injury on Sunday against the Houston Rockets, according to Shams Charania of ESPN.</p>
<p>"Stephon Curry has a goal to return to action on Sunday at home against the Houston Rockets. That's what he wants, that's his desire. But the next several days are going to be a vital period to see how does his knee respond to 5-on-5, to additional workouts," said Charania. </p>
<p>Charania added that if Curry couldn't make it back Sunday, the next opportunity would be Tuesday against the Kings.</p>
<p>Curry would have missed 27 straight games with a knee injury if he returns on Sunday.</p>
<p>Curry has not played since January 30.</p>
<p>"He's had multiple starts and stops throughout this ... but this does feel different internally with the Warriors because now Steph finally got in that 5-on-5 scrimmage. He's encouraged," said Charania.</p>
<p>The Warriors view this weekend as a "make or break time" for either Curry to return or get shut down for the season.</p></description>

      <link>https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/284842/NBA-Fines-Blazers-$100000-Suspends-Two-Executives-For-Illegal-Contact-With-Yang-Hansen</link>
      <description><p> </p>
<p>The NBA fined the Portland Trail Blazers $100,000 on Wednesday and suspended two team executives for improper contact made with center Yang Hansen in December 2023, nearly two years before the 7-foot Chinese big man was eligible for the draft.</p>
<p>Assistant general managers Mike Schmitz and Sergi Oliva each received two-week unpaid suspensions. The league ruled that both the franchise and the two officials violated rules governing contact with draft-ineligible players.</p>
<p>Portland self-reported the violation and cooperated fully with the league's investigation.</p>
<p>"When this was brought to our attention the Portland Trail Blazers self-reported to the NBA," the team said in a statement. "The team cooperated fully with the investigation and accept the league's determination."</p>
<p>Hansen was selected 16th overall by the Memphis Grizzlies in the 2025 draft. Portland acquired his rights the same night via a draft-night trade. Prior to entering the NBA, Hansen spent two seasons with the Qingdao Eagles in China's top professional league.</p>
<p>The 21-year-old has appeared in 41 games as a rookie this season for the Blazers, averaging 2.3 points and 1.6 rebounds in 7.2 minutes per contest.</p></description>

      <title>Bill Self Commits To Kansas For 2026-27 Season After Health Concerns</title>
      <link>https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/284841/Bill-Self-Commits-To-Kansas-For-2026-27-Season-After-Health-Concerns</link>
      <description><p>Kansas head coach Bill Self announced Wednesday he will return to coach the Jayhawks next season, ending weeks of uncertainty surrounding his future following a series of health concerns. Self said the decision came "with renewed clarity and the ongoing support from our administration."</p>
<p>"I remain focused and committed to Kansas and competing for a National Championship," Self said in a statement. "I look forward to seeing and hearing the best fans in college basketball next season at Allen Fieldhouse."</p>
<p>Self, 63, spent considerable time this week consulting with those close to him before reaching his decision. His announcement arrives more than a week after he indicated he would discuss his coaching future with family following multiple medical episodes in recent years.</p>
<p>The Hall of Fame coach has required cardiac intervention on two separate occasions. In 2023, he underwent a heart catheterization and had two stents placed after being hospitalized with chest tightness and balance concerns, forcing him to miss the Big 12 and NCAA tournaments. Last July, he was hospitalized again after experiencing concerning symptoms and had two additional stents inserted before being released two days later. In January, he missed a road game at Colorado after being taken to the hospital as a precaution.</p>
<p>Self acknowledged after Kansas's season-ending NCAA tournament loss to St. John's that a return was not guaranteed.</p>
<p>"I've had, obviously, some issues off the court healthwise. And that will be discussed," he said at the time. "But I love what I do. I want to feel good while I'm doing it, though."</p>
<p>His return carries considerable weight for the program and the sport. Self has compiled 855 wins across 33 seasons at Oral Roberts, Tulsa, Illinois and Kansas. Since arriving in Lawrence in 2003, he has won two national championships and reached four Final Fours.</p>
<p>The announcement arrives just days before the NCAA transfer portal opens Tuesday, the day after the national championship game.</p></description>

   </item>

      <title>Breanna Stewart, Sabrina Ionescu Both Announce Plans To Re-Sign With Liberty</title>
      <link>https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/284840/Breanna-Stewart-Sabrina-Ionescu-Both-Announce-Plans-To-Re-Sign-With-Liberty</link>
      <description><p>Two-time WNBA MVP Breanna Stewart announced she will remain with the New York Liberty in free agency, keeping the league's most decorated roster largely intact heading into the 2026 season. Teammate Sabrina Ionescu separately confirmed her intention to re-sign with New York on Wednesday at USA Basketball training camp.</p>
<p>"I'm gonna just set the record straight here: I will be staying in New York," Stewart said on her podcast, "Game Recognize Game with Stewie and Myles." "I'm not planning on taking any free agency meetings, even though I am an unrestricted free agent. My family is set up here, we're solid here. I'm going to be back in New York and that's all there is to it."</p>
<p>Stewart, a seven-time All-Star and three-time Liberty champion, averaged 18.3 points, 6.5 rebounds, 3.5 assists and 1.4 steals in 2025 before battling injuries late in the season and playoffs. She went on to lead her Mist BC squad to a title at Unrivaled, the 3-on-3 league she co-founded with Napheesa Collier.</p>
<p>Ionescu, a four-time All-Star who averaged 18.2 points, 5.7 assists and 4.9 rebounds last season, expressed confidence in the team's direction under first-year head coach Chris DeMarco.</p>
<p>"Obviously I'm where I'm supposed to be," Ionescu said. "Never thought anything different, and so excited to be able to sign and get started."</p>
<p>The Liberty also hold negotiating rights to several other key free agents including Jonquel Jones, Natasha Cloud, Emma Meesseman, Kennedy Burke and Betnijah Laney-Hamilton.</p>
<p>The exact free agency timeline remains unconfirmed as the league's new collective bargaining agreement is still being finalized. The expansion draft is scheduled for Friday with training camp set to begin April 19, creating an unusually compressed decision window for players and teams leaguewide.</p>
<p>"It's going to be real quick-hitting, and there's obviously a lot of money on the table," Ionescu said. "I think people are also going to have that to consider and they didn't always have that."</p></description>

   <item>
      <title>NBA's Gap Between Playoff, Lottery Teams Reaches Historic Levels Exposing Two-League Problems</title>
      <link>https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/284839/NBAs-Gap-Between-Playoff-Lottery-Teams-Reaches-Historic-Levels-Exposing-Two-League-Problems</link>
      <description><p>The divide between playoff contenders and lottery-bound teams has reached unprecedented levels this season, exposing a structural problem that is fundamentally altering the competitive landscape of the NBA, <a href="https://sports.yahoo.com/nba/article/welcome-to-the-nbas-new-a-league-and-b-league-phenomenon-174126322.html">according to a report by Yahoo's Tom Haberstroh</a>. The combined gap between the No. 10 and No. 11 seeds in each conference has ballooned to 20.5 games, nearly double the previous record this century.</p>
<p>In the Eastern Conference, the No. 10-seeded Charlotte Hornets hold a 9.5-game cushion over the No. 11 Milwaukee Bucks. Out West, the Golden State Warriors lead the Memphis Grizzlies by 11 games for the final play-in spot.</p>
<p>The numbers are historical as the previous largest combined gap this century stood at 11 games in 2018-19. This season's 20.5-game chasm represents by far the widest separation since the NBA adopted its 16-team playoff format in 1984.</p>
<p>The play-in tournament, introduced as an anti-tanking measure, appears to have accelerated the problem rather than solved it. In the first full 82-game season following its introduction, the combined gap between No. 10 and No. 11 seeds was seven games. It has climbed steadily since, reaching nine games in 2022-23, nine again in 2023-24 and ten in 2024-25, before this season's dramatic surge. The average gap in the play-in era is 11 games, compared to just 4.8 games across the previous ten seasons.</p>
<p>Nine teams currently operate as de facto non-competitors, posting a combined 12-167 record against playoff-caliber opponents across recent stretches of play. In March alone, those nine teams have gone 12-98, a .109 winning percentage, against postseason contenders.</p>
<p>The Washington Wizards have lost 20 consecutive games against winning teams. The Brooklyn Nets are 1-29 in its last 30 such contests. Dallas stands at 2-23 in its last 25.</p>
<p>Two factors are widely cited as accelerants. <a href="https://basketball.realgm.com/nba/draft/lottery_winners">The leaps by the Dallas Mavericks from the No. 11 lottery position to the No. 1 overall pick in 2025 and the Atlanta Hawks from 10th in consecutive recent drafts demonstrated that long-shot lottery odds can pay off spectacularly</a>. Combined with a strong 2026 draft class, the cost-benefit calculation for struggling franchises increasingly favors chasing lottery balls over competing for a play-in berth. Only one of ten No. 10 seeds in the play-in era has successfully advanced to the full playoffs.</p>
<p><a href="https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/284708/NBA-Presented-Three-Draft-Lottery-Reform-Options-To-Board-Of-Governors">The NBA is proposing three potential lottery reforms in an attempt to disincentivize tanking</a>.</p></description>

   <item>
      <title>Knicks, Wizards Among Teams That Tried To Trade Up For Jaden Ivey At 2022 NBA Draft</title>
      <link>https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/284838/Knicks-Wizards-Among-Teams-That-Tried-To-Trade-Up-For-Jaden-Ivey-At-2022-NBA-Draft</link>
      <description><p>The New York Knicks and Washington Wizards were among multiple teams that aggressively pursued Jaden Ivey at the 2022 NBA Draft, according to league sources. Detroit's phones were flooded with trade inquiries centered on the fifth overall pick before the Pistons ultimately kept him.</p>
<p>Detroit elected to stay put and select Ivey despite the volume of interest. The Pistons then acquired center Jalen Duren later in the lottery via trade, securing both targets on draft night.</p>
<p>There had been internal consideration that Ivey could be moved at the draft, sources said, but the front office under then-general manager Troy Weaver found a way to retain both prospects. Weaver viewed Duren as his primary objective entering the draft and structured his draft-night maneuvering around landing the Memphis center regardless of outcome.</p>
<p>At the time, the Sacramento Kings received considerable consternation for selecting Keegan Murray over Ivey. Murray has become a solid two-way player for the Kings and <a href="https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/282314/Keegan-Murray-Kings-Agree-To-Five-Year-$140M-Extension">signed a five-year, $140 million rookie contract extension in October</a>. </p>
<p>The Pistons envisioned Ivey as the long-term backcourt complement to franchise cornerstone Cade Cunningham, a dynamic, high-energy foil to Cunningham's methodical style. Ivey made the <a href="https://basketball.realgm.com/nba/awards/by-type/All-Rookie-First-Team/18">All-Rookie Team</a> in his first season, averaging 16.3 points and 5.2 assists across 74 games.</p>
<p>Ivey was traded to the Chicago Bulls at February's deadline and appeared in just four games before being waived Monday for conduct detrimental to the team. He will receive his full $10.1 million salary and becomes an unrestricted free agent once he clears waivers.</p></description>
      <pubDate>Wed, 01 Apr 2026 15:21:03 EDT</pubDate>
      <guid>https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/284838/Knicks-Wizards-Among-Teams-That-Tried-To-Trade-Up-For-Jaden-Ivey-At-2022-NBA-Draft</guid>
      <media:thumbnail url="https://basketball.realgm.com/images/nba/4.2/wiretap/photos/2006/Ivey_Jaden_det_221114.jpg" />
   </item>
   <item>
      <title>Zion Williamson Expected To Be Put On Trade Block By Pelicans In Offseason</title>
      <link>https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/284837/Zion-Williamson-Expected-To-Be-Put-On-Trade-Block-By-Pelicans-In-Offseason</link>
      <description><p>Multiple executives around the NBA expect the New Orleans Pelicans to listen to trade offers on Zion Williamson, who becomes extension-eligible this offseason. The two-time All-Star says he is aware of the speculation and acknowledges the business reality he faces.</p>
<p>"New Orleans is home for me. It's where I want to be," Williamson told Yahoo Sports. "But at the end of the day, if we're going to be realistic about it, the NBA is a business. I could be traded in the offseason, or I could be traded before [next season's] trade deadline. Not that I want that to happen. But that's just the realism of it."</p>
<p>The trade chatter stems largely from a front office decision made nine months ago. Joe Dumars and Troy Weaver surrendered the 23rd pick and <a href="https://basketball.realgm.com/nba/draft/future_drafts/detailed">an unprotected 2026 first-rounder</a> to move up to 13th in the draft and select Maryland forward Derik Queen, a poor defensive player whose fit alongside Williamson has proven deeply problematic.</p>
<p>Interim coach James Borrego has largely abandoned pairing the two after dismal early-season results. The Pelicans went 15-41 before the All-Star break but have posted a 10-10 record since separating the combination.</p>
<p>The unprotected 2026 first-rounder owed to Atlanta currently carries a 32% chance of landing in the top four of what scouts consider a strong draft class, raising the stakes of the Queen gamble considerably.</p>
<p>Williamson, who turns 26 in July, ranks among the league's most productive isolation scorers this season and has stayed largely healthy. However, skepticism persists leaguewide given his injury history, limited defensive impact and a contract guaranteeing $42.2 million next season.</p>
<p>"Right now he's a borderline All-Star who makes a lot of money," one Western Conference executive said. "That's the sort of player a lot of teams are now avoiding."</p>
<p>Sources close to Williamson say neither he nor his representatives have received any indication from New Orleans that a move is imminent.</p></description>
      <pubDate>Wed, 01 Apr 2026 15:12:43 EDT</pubDate>
      <guid>https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/284837/Zion-Williamson-Expected-To-Be-Put-On-Trade-Block-By-Pelicans-In-Offseason</guid>
      <media:thumbnail url="https://basketball.realgm.com/images/nba/4.2/wiretap/photos/2006/Williamson_Zion_nop_240325.jpg" />
   </item>
